propositions: The rebar-gerrit-alerts SNS email subscription must be confirmed (PendingConfirmation=false) so CloudWatch alarms actually deliver notifications.
No terraform drift-detection CI exists, so committed-but-unapplied HCL can silently sit unapplied — the dc3f root cause.
A new drift-detection GitHub workflow runs terraform plan with -detailed-exitcode via GitHub OIDC to a plan-only role, failing on any non-empty plan.
A least-privilege plan-only IAM role rebar-terraform-plan is added with ReadOnlyAccess plus KMS decrypt scoped to SSM.
A live-but-unmanaged alarm rebar-gerrit-data-disk-high has an empty alarm_actions list and never notifies; it must be codified in HCL, wired to the SNS topic, and imported into state.
Terraform writes to the shared S3 state backend must follow the HCL merge, so import/apply is a targeted post-merge operator step to avoid sweeping unrelated instance user_data drift.
